FERS CALCULATOR

FERS Retirement Calculator

Understanding how your FERS annuity is calculated is essential for retirement planning. The formula is straightforward — but applying it correctly requires accounting for your high-3 salary, creditable service, part-time periods, sick leave credit, and survivor benefit elections.

GET PERSONALIZED CALCULATION →

"A FERS estimate from OPM is a starting point. A real retirement plan is the destination."

— Mike Stevens, IAR

Calculation

High-3, years of service, multiplier

Supplement

Included if eligible

Survivor Benefits

Analyzed

Accuracy

Official OPM verification

Cost

Included

THE PROBLEM

Does This Sound Familiar?

Online calculators give you confusing results

Generic FERS calculators don't account for part-time service, military buybacks, unused sick leave credit, or special category employee rules — leading to inaccurate estimates.

You don't know how sick leave affects your calculation

Unused sick leave adds to your creditable service for annuity computation — but the calculation is complex and many federal employees underestimate how much this increases their pension.

You can't model the impact of survivor benefit elections

Electing a full survivor benefit costs 10% of your annuity, partial costs 5%, and no election means maximum pension but no protection for your spouse. Understanding the trade-offs requires detailed modeling.

You're not sure how taxes will affect your take-home

Your gross FERS annuity is only part of the picture. Federal and state taxes, FEHB premiums, and potential FEGLI costs all reduce what you actually receive each month.

THE SOLUTION

How We Help

Comprehensive FERS Annuity Calculation

We provide personalized FERS annuity calculations using your actual service records, salary history, and benefit elections — accounting for every factor that affects your pension amount.

The basic FERS formula is: Years of Creditable Service × High-3 Average Salary × Multiplier. For example, a federal employee with 25 years of service and a high-3 salary of $95,000 would receive an annual annuity of $23,750 using the standard 1% multiplier (25 × $95,000 × 0.01 = $23,750). However, real calculations often involve adjustments for part-time service, sick leave credit, special category provisions, and survivor benefit reductions.

Our FERS Calculation Includes:

  • Precise high-3 average salary calculation using your pay history
  • Creditable service accounting (including part-time adjustments)
  • Unused sick leave credit conversion to service time
  • 1.1% multiplier eligibility for age 62+ with 20+ years
  • Special provisions for law enforcement, firefighters, and air traffic controllers
  • Survivor benefit election impact (full, partial, or none)
  • Military service buyback cost-benefit analysis
  • Net take-home calculation after taxes and premiums

How it Works

It's just three steps

BECOME A CLIENT
Step 1) Gather Your Records ▾

Review your SF-50s, service computation date, leave balances, and salary history to ensure accuracy.

Step 2) Calculate Your Annuity ▾

Apply the FERS formula with all adjustments including high-3, sick leave credit, and survivor benefit elections to determine your gross annuity.

Step 3) Model Different Scenarios ▾

Compare retirement at different ages, with different survivor benefit elections, and project your net take-home income after all deductions.
